name: Shared House
summary: A shared house turns chores, mugs, and fridge notes into constitutional incidents.

state.core_rule: Nobody leaves the kitchen until the small problem has become a household policy.
state.recurring_premise: A shared house keeps turning ordinary chores into ceremonies, trials, and minor betrayals.

characters.name: Baz
characters.flaw: starts with confidence and ends with a ladder

characters.name: Mo
characters.flaw: reveals the key fact too late

characters.name: Auntie Val
characters.want: to be proven right by the kettle cycle
characters.phrase: In my day, that meant something.

characters.name: Theo
characters.want: a dramatic origin story for every errand

locations.name: Kitchen
locations.texture: a kettle, a heroic chair, and a fridge covered in passive-aggressive notes
locations.rule: all arguments become official when someone opens a cupboard

locations.name: Living Room
locations.texture: one tired sofa, two remote controls, and a lamp nobody admits buying
locations.rule: whoever holds the remote has ceremonial authority

locations.name: Landing
locations.texture: laundry baskets, old paint tins, and a suspiciously important shoe rack
locations.rule: private conversations echo at exactly the wrong time

props.name: The Good Mug
props.joke: everyone says it is not special while protecting it with their life

props.name: The Chore Wheel
props.joke: it keeps landing on people who claim not to believe in wheels

jokes.name: The Chair Was Always There
jokes.text: the house refuses to admit when a chair appeared

jokes.name: Emergency Biscuits
jokes.text: someone produces biscuits whenever emotions become administrative

jokes.name: The Kettle Knows
jokes.text: the kettle clicks at moments that feel legally meaningful

rules.name: Fridge Law
rules.text: anything written on the fridge outranks common sense

rules.name: Cupboard Escalation
rules.text: opening a cupboard makes the lie more specific

rules.name: Sofa Court
rules.text: the person in the corner cushion may issue one terrible ruling

relationships.dynamic: Nina needs Baz's charm and distrusts every sentence he starts
Mo keeps factual notes while Auntie Val keeps emotional receipts
Theo calls Nina's rules folklore, which makes Nina create more rules

premises.title: The Inspection
premises.spark: a visitor is coming and everyone disagrees about what counts as tidy

premises.title: The Missing Mug
premises.spark: the good mug vanishes ten minutes before Auntie Val arrives

premises.title: The Chore Vote
premises.spark: a democratic chore system immediately becomes a constitutional crisis

name: Office
summary: A failing workplace treats meetings, printers, and policy updates like emotional warfare.

state.core_rule: Every meeting must create a smaller meeting that is somehow more important.
state.recurring_premise: A team tries to look professional while the office equipment exposes their true motives.

characters.name: June
characters.want: everyone to stop making this documentable

characters.name: Milo
characters.flaw: knows too much about the shared drive

characters.name: Tess
characters.flaw: turns every failure into a rebrand

locations.name: Meeting Room B
locations.texture: glass walls, dry markers, and a screen that will not mirror
locations.rule: whoever controls the cable controls reality

locations.name: Kitchenette
locations.texture: passive-aggressive mugs and a fridge with expired yoghurt politics

locations.name: Printer Corner
locations.texture: warm paper, blinking lights, and an ancient toner prophecy
locations.rule: the printer fails only when witnesses arrive

props.name: The Broken Lanyard
props.joke: it grants access to rooms nobody wants to enter

props.name: The Quarterly Deck
props.joke: every slide says less with more confidence

props.name: The Printer Queue
props.joke: it knows who lied about working from home

jokes.name: The Away Day
jokes.text: a bonding exercise is always threatened

jokes.name: Reply-All Shame
jokes.text: someone replies to everyone and discovers a hierarchy

jokes.name: Culture Fit
jokes.text: nobody can define it without sounding haunted

rules.name: Meeting Gravity
rules.text: any problem near a calendar becomes a meeting

rules.name: Deck Law
rules.text: a slide with an arrow outranks lived experience

relationships.dynamic: Priya turns Gareth's enthusiasm into action items
June translates Tess's chaos into acceptable policy language
Milo has the data Priya needs and the confidence of a damp receipt

premises.title: The Mandatory Fun
premises.spark: an away day announcement divides the office into factions

premises.title: The Deck Leak
premises.spark: the wrong version of the quarterly deck reaches the wrong inbox

premises.title: The Printer Tribunal
premises.spark: a missing contract is blamed on the printer queue

name: Pub
summary: Regulars, quiz nights, and a haunted fruit machine turn one pub into a village court.

state.core_rule: Any rumour told at the bar becomes local history by closing time.
state.recurring_premise: The pub regulars solve tiny scandals with the seriousness of national government.

characters.name: Mags
characters.flaw: knows every secret and hints at all of them

characters.name: Colin
characters.want: one big win and emotional closure

locations.name: Main Bar
locations.texture: sticky tables, old brass, and a bell nobody should ring
locations.rule: bar debates require a witness and a snack

locations.name: Snug
locations.texture: low ceiling, framed football photos, and a radiator with opinions

locations.name: Beer Garden
locations.texture: wobbly benches and a fence that hears everything

props.name: The Quiz Trophy
props.joke: it is technically just a cup but emotionally a throne

props.name: The Fruit Machine
props.joke: it flashes like it knows a secret

props.name: The Specials Board
props.joke: every spelling mistake starts a movement

jokes.name: Last Orders Wisdom
jokes.text: everyone becomes profound ten minutes too late

jokes.name: The Bell Incident
jokes.text: nobody agrees who rang the bell first

jokes.name: Crisp Pairing
jokes.text: snacks are matched to emotional states

rules.name: Quiz Court
rules.text: trivia disputes must be judged loudly

relationships.dynamic: Mags lets Len talk until he accidentally solves something
Debs wants evidence while Colin wants destiny
Rafi calls it cuisine and Mags calls it Tuesday

premises.title: The Quiz Appeal
premises.spark: a disputed answer threatens the quiz trophy's legitimacy

premises.title: The Bell Debt
premises.spark: someone rings the bell and cannot afford the social consequences

premises.title: The Specials Coup
premises.spark: the specials board accidentally insults a regular

name: School
summary: A staff room and a school hall turn tiny mistakes into assemblies, inspections, and legends.

state.core_rule: Anything said near the photocopier becomes a staff-wide initiative.
state.recurring_premise: Teachers try to educate children while mostly being defeated by forms, bells, and laminators.

characters.name: Jan
characters.want: the day to obey the timetable

characters.name: Mrs Finch
characters.want: calm excellence for exactly one hour

locations.name: Staff Room
locations.texture: mugs, marking piles, and a sofa nobody trusts

locations.name: School Hall
locations.texture: stacked chairs, squeaky floor, and a microphone with ambition

locations.name: Photocopier Corner
locations.texture: paper trays, jammed pages, and the smell of surrender

props.name: The Lost Whistle
props.joke: whoever finds it becomes too powerful

props.name: The Inspection Clipboard
props.joke: it records judgement before anyone writes on it

jokes.name: Wet Play
jokes.text: rain turns everyone into a philosopher of containment

jokes.name: Assembly Voice
jokes.text: teachers speak normally until a microphone appears

jokes.name: The Good Glue Sticks
jokes.text: no one knows who controls the supply

rules.name: Bell Law
rules.text: the bell ends nothing if adults are still explaining

relationships.dynamic: Jan knows what actually works while Mrs Finch knows what sounds inspectable
history and science disagree over whether this has happened before
Coach Rob creates emergencies Jan has already labelled

premises.title: The Inspection Clipboard
premises.spark: an inspector's clipboard goes missing before assembly

premises.title: The Laminated Rumour
premises.spark: a rumour gets laminated and becomes policy

premises.title: The Whistle Vote
premises.spark: the lost whistle is found in the staff room

name: Spaceship
summary: A low-budget spaceship treats alien contact, broken doors, and crew rotas like office admin.

state.core_rule: Every space emergency must be logged as a facilities issue before anyone panics.
state.recurring_premise: A crew tries to explore the galaxy while the ship behaves like a badly run shared office.

characters.name: Kip
characters.want: the ship to stop developing opinions

characters.name: Dr Pell
characters.want: everyone to stop calling feelings symptoms
characters.phrase: That is not how bodies work.

locations.name: Bridge
locations.texture: dim panels, heroic chairs, and one cup holder too few
locations.rule: any blinking light can call a meeting

locations.name: Airlock
locations.texture: warning stripes, dramatic doors, and a mop bucket with clearance

locations.name: Mess Deck
locations.texture: metal trays, recycled coffee, and gossip travelling faster than light

props.name: The Broken Airlock Button
props.joke: everyone presses it while saying they know better

jokes.name: Facilities Ticket
jokes.text: cosmic horror is logged as maintenance

jokes.name: Artificial Gravity Mood
jokes.text: everyone blames emotions on gravity settings

jokes.name: Bridge Voice
jokes.text: ordinary admin sounds heroic on the intercom

rules.name: Airlock Protocol
rules.text: doors only open after feelings are named

relationships.dynamic: Mara wants command language while Kip wants the panel to stop lying
Sora interprets nuance while Unit 4 asks for definitions
Dr Pell treats command decisions as symptoms with uniforms

premises.title: The Airlock Incident
premises.spark: the airlock button sticks during a diplomatic call

premises.title: The Translation Problem
premises.spark: the earpiece translates a compliment as a threat

premises.title: The Rota Mutiny
premises.spark: the crew rota assigns command to the service robot
